surface.name: Get started with Claude
surface.selection_basis: Anthropic places Get started with Claude under First steps, and the API overview explicitly directs new direct-model-access developers to this guide. The guide names and demonstrates the terminal result as the first Claude API call.

alternatives_considered.name: Claude Managed Agents quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The API overview routes managed-agent infrastructure to this separate surface and recommends the selected Get started guide for direct model access.

alternatives_considered.name: Workbench
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The API overview presents Workbench as an in-browser way to try the API, while the selected First steps quickstart explicitly produces the first programmatic call.

alternatives_considered.name: Cloud platform access
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Foundry are alternatives for existing cloud commitments, IAM, compliance, or consolidated billing; Anthropic says the direct API is best for new integrations and full feature access.

entry_point.developer_intent: Make the first direct Messages API call to Claude after selecting Anthropic.
assumed_prior_state: Discovery is complete and the developer has selected Anthropic's direct Claude API.
The developer does not yet have a funded Console organization, API key, or configured client.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: You made your first API call
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The developer has sent the quickstart's renewable-energy prompt to the Claude Messages API and received Claude's assistant message.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The cURL route returns a JSON message with type message, role assistant, a text content block beginning with search strategies for renewable energy, stop_reason end_turn, and token usage; SDK and CLI routes print the equivalent assistant text.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: After the documented request and output, the Next steps section states You made your first API call.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Anthropic explicitly labels the completed request as the first API call. Messages API patterns, model comparison, features, SDK reference, and the guide's aspirational web-search-assistant build are placed after this statement.

prerequisites.requirement: An Anthropic Claude Console account and organization are required for the direct API path.
A new Console organization must have prepaid usage credits before an API call can succeed; organization and use-case information plus payment details are required to fund it.
An API key generated in Console Account Settings is required for the quickstart's direct API request.
The cURL route requires a terminal with cURL. The CLI and seven SDK routes require their documented runtime, build tool, package manager, or Homebrew installation prerequisites.
The developer must be in a country or region supported by the Claude API.

primary_path.action: Open Get started with Claude and confirm the direct Messages API path.
details: The guide's stated purpose is to make the first API call to Claude.
Its prerequisite section requires a Console account and API key.
primary_path.input: Intent to build with Claude directly
primary_path.success_signal: The Call the API section and language tabs are visible.

primary_path.action: Create or log in to a Claude Console account and create or join a Console organization.
details: Console is where organizations create keys, add team users, configure billing, and use Workbench.
API access is subject to Anthropic's Commercial Terms.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The public docs do not enumerate the signup form, identity verification, email verification, or organization-creation screens.

primary_path.action: Fill out the requested organization and use-case information.
primary_path.success_signal: The billing flow accepts the organization and use-case information.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The help article does not enumerate the fields or any review time.

primary_path.action: Enter payment details in the organization billing flow.
details: An Admin or Billing role can later update the payment method under Settings, Billing, by selecting the pencil icon, entering new card details, and selecting Update.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A billing or payment problem can cause a 402 billing_error on the API request.

primary_path.action: Purchase prepaid usage credits for the organization.
details: Navigate to the Billing page, select Buy credits, enter the credit amount, and complete the purchase.
Purchased credits become available immediately, expire after one year, cannot be extended, and are non-refundable.
Auto-reload is optional and can be configured later.
primary_path.input: Payment method and selected credit amount
primary_path.success_signal: The credits appear as available on the Billing page.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Without credits, API and Workbench calls are unavailable.

primary_path.action: Create an API key in Console Account Settings.
details: Open the API keys page from the profile and select Create Key.
Choose the key's expiration when creating it.
A workspace can be used to segment keys and control spend by use case.
primary_path.input: Funded Console organization and key expiration choice
primary_path.success_signal: The key value is displayed for secure storage.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Organization or workspace permissions can prevent key creation or access to a model resource.

primary_path.action: Copy the key to secure storage and export it as ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.
details: Run export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your-api-key-here for the cURL route.
Avoid version control and use a secrets-management system for durable storage.
primary_path.success_signal: The shell can substitute the key into the request header.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A malformed, revoked, or expired key returns a 401 authentication_error.

primary_path.action: Send the documented POST request to https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages with cURL.
details: Set content-type application/json, x-api-key from ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, and anthropic-version 2023-06-01.
Send model claude-opus-4-8, max_tokens 1000, and the user prompt asking what to search for to find the latest renewable-energy developments.
primary_path.input: Funded account, environment key, and request body
primary_path.success_signal: The request returns a message object instead of an API error.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: 400 indicates invalid request content; 402 billing; 403 permission; 404 endpoint or resource; 429 rate limit; 500 should be retried with exponential backoff; 504 timed out; 529 temporarily overloaded.

primary_path.action: Verify the returned JSON contains Claude's assistant message and completed turn.
details: The demonstrated response includes model and message id, type message, role assistant, a text content block with renewable-energy search strategies, stop_reason end_turn, and input and output usage.
primary_path.success_signal: The assistant text and end_turn terminal state match the demonstrated response shape, and the next section says the first API call was made.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An error response includes an error type, message, and request_id; use the request ID when contacting support.

branches.condition: The developer uses an existing organization rather than creating one.
branches.path: Log in or accept organization access, confirm the role permits billing and key creation as needed, then continue with existing funding or add credits.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Can remove organization creation and payment setup, but team role and available-credit gates remain.

branches.condition: The organization uses a paid API contract with monthly invoicing.
branches.path: Use the contracted invoicing and payment process instead of the standard new-account prepaid-credit flow.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes the billing path and may require contacting Anthropic for custom limits, invoicing, or support.

branches.condition: The organization uses Workload Identity Federation.
branches.path: Configure the federation rule and authenticate with a short-lived bearer token from POST /v1/oauth/token instead of an API key.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces static key generation and x-api-key with organization identity configuration and Authorization Bearer token handling.

branches.condition: The developer chooses the ant CLI tab.
branches.path: Install with brew install anthropics/tap/ant or another CLI installation method; run ant auth login, authorize the browser OAuth flow, then run ant auth status. On a remote host use --no-browser, open the URL on another device, and paste the returned code. Then run ant messages create with the quickstart request. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, if set, takes precedence over login credentials.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Uses CLI OAuth and a CLI-native request instead of direct cURL headers; adds installation, browser authorization, and credential-status verification.

branches.path: Export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY; create and enter claude-quickstart; create and activate .venv; install anthropic; create quickstart.py with Anthropic().messages.create and the documented prompt; run python quickstart.py; verify printed assistant text.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds a Python virtual environment, SDK installation, code file, and run command.

branches.path: Export the key; create and enter claude-quickstart; run npm init -y, set package type module, and install @anthropic-ai/sdk; create quickstart.ts with the Messages request; run npx tsx quickstart.ts; verify assistant text.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds Node package setup, TypeScript code, and the tsx runner.

branches.path: Export the key; run dotnet new console -n ClaudeQuickstart, enter the directory, and add the Anthropic package; replace the program with the documented Messages request; run the .NET project and verify assistant text.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds .NET project creation, NuGet installation, C# code, and compilation.

branches.path: Export the key; create and enter claude-quickstart; initialize the Go module and get github.com/anthropics/anthropic-sdk-go; create the documented Go Messages program; run it and verify assistant text.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds Go module initialization, SDK retrieval, source code, and compilation.

branches.path: Install JDK 25 or later plus Gradle or Maven; create the quickstart project and the documented build.gradle.kts or Maven configuration and QuickStart source; run gradle run or mvn compile exec:java -Dexec.mainClass=QuickStart; verify assistant text.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds a JDK and a Gradle-versus-Maven build branch with project files and compilation.

branches.path: Export the key; create and enter claude-quickstart; install anthropic-ai/sdk and guzzlehttp/guzzle:^7 with Composer; create quickstart.php with the documented Messages request; run php quickstart.php; verify assistant text.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds PHP, Composer dependencies, a PHP file, and execution.

branches.path: Export the key; create the quickstart project and documented Ruby dependency setup; create quickstart.rb with the Messages request; run bundle exec ruby quickstart.rb; verify assistant text.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds Ruby, Bundler dependency setup, a Ruby file, and execution.

branches.condition: The developer chooses AWS, Google Cloud, or Microsoft Foundry access.
branches.path: Follow the platform-specific documentation and use that provider's IAM, credential, billing, availability, and endpoint path instead of Console funding and direct API-key authentication.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces the direct Anthropic account, billing, and authentication journey with a cloud-provider-specific one.

friction_gates.description: The direct API requires a Console account and organization, subject to Commercial Terms and regional availability.
A new organization must supply additional organization and use-case information before funding.
The standard new-account path requires payment details and prepaid credit purchase before API use.
A key must be created with an expiration choice, securely stored, and exposed to the runtime; workspace permissions can constrain it.
The quickstart offers cURL, ant CLI, Python, TypeScript, C#, Go, Java, PHP, and Ruby routes with materially different installation and project setup.
The organization has automatic usage-tier spend and rate limits; 429 and acceleration limits can block a request.

sources.title: How can I access the Claude API?
sections_used: How can I access the Claude API?

sources.title: How do I pay for my Claude API usage?
sections_used: How to update your Console payment method

sources.title: I created a Claude Console organization: how do I start using the Claude API?
What should I do if I suspect my API key has been compromised?
evidence_supported: API keys page and Create Key control

sources.title: Claude API errors
evidence_supported: Authentication, billing, permission, validation, rate, retry, timeout, and overloaded failures
Error response shape and support request ID

uncertainties.question: What exact identity, email-verification, and organization-creation screens does a brand-new Console user encounter?
uncertainties.impact: These screens can add steps or waiting before organization information and billing.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Official docs state that a Console organization must be created but do not publish the full signup UI sequence.

uncertainties.question: Does Anthropic intend one quickstart client as the canonical default?
uncertainties.impact: cURL has two runtime steps, CLI uses browser OAuth, and seven SDK routes add different toolchains and project files.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The page presents all nine tabs without calling one recommended or default; cURL is used as the recorded primary because it is the first rendered tab and its complete response is demonstrated, while every other route is preserved as a branch.

excluded_after_success: Building the web search assistant named in the page description
Production retries, streaming, and error handling
